Title: Oryza sativa PECTIN DEFECTIVE TAPETUM1 affects anther development through a pectin-mediated signaling pathway in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Wall-associated kinase (WAK) family receptor-like kinase; extracellular domain binds pectic polysaccharides
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secretory vesicle compartment; cell periphery; endoplasmic reticulum-associated irregular features
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks at anther stage 8a–8b; detected in dyad cells and tapetum at stage 8a
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peaks at anther stage 8a–8b; detected in dyad cells and tapetum at stage 8a
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall component and signaling molecule involved in tapetal programmed cell death and pollen wall development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsiWAK1 is required for proper tapetal PCD timing; RNAi causes earlier tapetal PCD, sterility, aborted pollen, and delayed anther dehiscence, while overexpression delays PCD and partially rescues premature PCD defects in ospdt1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference; overexpression; rescue in ospdt1 background; pectin-binding assay; transient expression/subcellular localization; TUNEL assay; cytology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsiWAK1 extracellular domain binds polygalacturonide in vitro. 2. Its localization to secretory vesicle compartment depends on the pectin-binding region and is reduced in ospdt1. 3. OsiWAK1 RNAi causes premature tapetal PCD and male sterility-like defects. 4. OsiWAK1 overexpression delays tapetal PCD and partially rescues ospdt1 premature PCD defects, indicating a pivotal role downstream of OsPDT1-mediated pectin function.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anther developmental stages 8a–14; WT, ospdt1 mutant, RNAi and overexpression backgrounds
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for OsiWAK1-eYFP overexpression; pTCK303 RNAi construct for knockdown
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering male fertility/sterility through modulation of tapetal PCD, but careful tuning is required because both knockdown and overexpression impair fertility.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Both reduced and increased OsiWAK1 activity cause pollen defects and sterility-related phenotypes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GAUT1-homolog; GAUT domain; type II membrane-associated glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream/midstream polymer synthesis step in Golgi-mediated pectin assembly
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: D-galacturonic acid-containing donor substrate(s) for HG polymerization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes transfer of galacturonosyl residues during homogalacturonan synthesis in pectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the structural/signaling pectin pool that influences downstream pectin-mediated signaling affecting anther development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not explicitly established as rate-limiting; shown as required for normal HG synthesis/function
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in root, stem, leaf, sheath, and spikelets; peaks at spikelet stages 8b–9; predominantly expressed in microspore mother cells, dyad cells, tetrads, microspores, and tapetal cells at stages 7–9
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in root, stem, leaf, sheath, and spikelets; peaks at spikelet stages 8b–9; predominantly expressed in microspore mother cells, dyad cells, tetrads, microspores, and tapetal cells at stages 7–9
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall integrity and pectin-mediated signaling linked to tapetal PCD and pollen wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of OsPDT1 reduces GalA content, disrupts pectin-mediated signaling, causes premature tapetal PCD, disordered pollen walls, and absolute male sterility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; mutant sequencing; complementation; HPLC measurement of galacturonic acid; subcellular localization; expression analysis; cytology; TUNEL assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsiWAK1 is functionally affected downstream through pectin-mediated signaling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDT1 was identified as Os09g0531900/LOC_Os09g36190 by map-based cloning of ospdt1. 2. The mutant carries a T-to-A transversion causing a truncated protein. 3. OsPDT1 is homologous to Arabidopsis GAUT1 and contributes to HG synthesis, as ospdt1 shows reduced GalA content. 4. OsPDT1 localizes to the Golgi apparatus. 5. Loss of OsPDT1 causes premature tapetal PCD, abnormal pollen wall formation, and male sterility, likely via altered pectin-mediated signaling affecting OsiWAK1.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anther developmental stages 7–14; EMS mutant background; complementation in ospdt1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsPDT1 genomic locus with upstream/downstream regions for complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite reduced GalA content, JIM5/JIM7 immunofluorescence detected no major change in structural HG deposition, suggesting OsPDT1 mainly affects a subtle pectin pool relevant to signaling rather than gross cell wall structure in anthers.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for controlling male fertility/sterility in rice hybrid breeding through manipulation of pectin biosynthesis in anthers.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes complete male sterility; no obvious vegetative growth defect reported.
